commit.message:
Update dependencies to fix security vulnerabilities

- minimist: ^1.2.5 → ^1.2.8 (fixes critical Prototype Pollution)
- lodash: ^4.17.21 → ^4.17.23 (fixes Prototype Pollution in unset/omit)
- genetical: switched to juanramirez/genetical fork which updates its
  internal lodash from 3.x to 4.x (fixes critical/high CVEs)

commit.message:
fix: add docs

* Feature/sober (#297)

* feat: add new endpoints for credit sources and new user registrations heatmap; enhance credit tracking in repositories

* feat: add model tier parameter to various functions for enhanced consumption tracking

* feat: migrate docs site into monorepo and integrate with Xyzen stack

- Copy docs (Next.js + MDX) from standalone repo to docs/
- Add basePath: '/docs' for nginx sub-path routing
- Add docs service to docker-compose (base + dev) with hot-reload
- Add /docs upstream and location block in nginx.conf
- Add docs build/merge/deploy jobs to release, beta, and ghcr-build workflows
- Add just commands: dev-docs, type-docs, lint-docs, build-docs
- Clean up non-Xyzen content (studio, protium, protocol, labos, development, en)
- Promote Xyzen docs to root (/ instead of /xyzen/)
- Simplify .gitignore and navItems for docs-only site

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add provider.quota_exhausted error code and default to Bedrock for Claude models

Upstream GPUGeek "没有可用token" errors were misclassified as system.internal_error.
Add a new PROVIDER_QUOTA_EXHAUSTED error code to properly categorize upstream provider
quota issues. Also swap ULTRA/PRO tier priorities and infer_provider_preference order
so Bedrock is the primary provider for Claude models, with GPUGeek as fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(docs): rewrite auth to use Xyzen backend OAuth via Casdoor

Replace legacy ScienceOL cookie-based auth with Xyzen's OAuth flow.
The docs site now shares localStorage tokens with Xyzen web, so users
logged into either app are automatically authenticated in both.

- Rewrite AuthService to use Xyzen endpoints (login/casdoor, validate)
- Auto-login on mount: handle OAuth callback code, then validate token
- Sign-in button triggers same-page Casdoor OAuth redirect
- Simplify DropdownMenu to username + sign out only
- Remove unused config exports (BASE_URL, WS_URL, Environment, etc.)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: embed code-server IDE in sandbox workspace, hide dock during immersive view

Replace the custom FileTree + read-only MonacoViewer with an embedded
code-server iframe for a full IDE experience (editing, terminal, search).

Backend: new POST /{session_id}/sandbox/ide endpoint that installs/starts
code-server on demand and returns a proxied preview URL with theme sync.

Frontend: SandboxWorkspace renders a loading → iframe → error flow with
theme-matched background to prevent white flash. BottomDock hides entirely
when dockMinimized is true for an unobstructed immersive workspace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: persist node outputs in node_end events for agentExecution reconstruction after refresh

Backend: include node output in tracer's node_end event data so
chat_event_handlers can store it via append_timeline_entry. Strip
output before SSE publish to avoid large payloads. Remove duplicate
timeline from streaming_end finalization (already appended incrementally).

Frontend: make reconstructAgentExecutionFromMetadata resilient to
missing node_outputs by falling back to node_order for phase creation.
Fix SharedChatPage to process messages through groupToolMessagesWithAssistant
so shared conversations render with proper agent execution timelines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(chat): migrate connection and message actions from WebSocket to SSE

Replace xyzenService WebSocket with sseClient for server→client events
and topicService REST for client→server actions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: SSE streaming, docs i18n, sandbox improvements, and UI updates

- Migrate chat from WebSocket to SSE + REST (new sseClient, topicService,
  Redis Streams backend, chat_actions/events endpoints)
- Remove legacy WS chat handler and related tests
- Docs: replace (cn) hardcoded routes with [locale] dynamic i18n routing
- Sandbox: improve runner backend lifecycle and nginx SSE proxy config
- Web: add skills button, mobile menu enhancements, spatial workspace
  agent tools, and streaming handler improvements
- Service: simplify system_agent, update agent repos, add auth middleware
  enhancements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add skills_auto to GraphConfig schema and PATCH guard, marketplace search UI

Backend:
- Add skills_auto field to GraphConfig (default=True) so Pydantic accepts it
- Extend non-editable agent PATCH guard to pass through skills_auto changes

Frontend:
- Move marketplace/skill search above tabs, always visible
- Adjust MCP settings form padding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct ChatMessageNav active dot when first message is visible

The IntersectionObserver's rootMargin excluded the top 30% of the viewport,
causing the first message to never be detected as active when scrolled to top.
Initialize activeId to the first user message and add a scroll listener to
force-activate it when near the top of the container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(chat): collapse large pasted text into expandable capsule, hide tools tab

- FloatingChatInput: detect pastes >500 chars or >8 lines and display
  as a collapsible capsule with preview, stats, edit, and remove
- Capsule: remove "tools" tab from TABS array and ToolsTab rendering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove invalid dotfiles option from serve.json causing CrashLoopBackOff

The "dotfiles" key is not a valid serve.json property and causes a schema
validation error on startup, crashing xyzen-web pods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: migrate topic rename to Redis Streams and remove dead WS code

topic_generator.py was still publishing topic_updated events via Redis
Pub/Sub (chat:{connection_id}), which SSE clients never receive. Switch
to XADD on the topic's Redis Stream so rename events reach all connected
SSE clients in real time.

Also clean up dead code left over from the WS→SSE migration:
- Delete xyzenService.ts (444-line dead WS client, only setBackendUrl
  was called; sseClient reads backendUrl from Zustand store directly)
- Remove staleWatchdogIds from chatSlice helpers (never populated)
- Remove unused connection_manager/connection_id params from
  get_ai_response_stream and its callers
- Remove stale xyzenService comment in http/client.ts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace serve with nginx for static file serving

serve CLI does not support dotfiles, causing .well-known paths to
return SPA fallback instead of the actual file. Switch to nginx:alpine
which handles dotfiles natively and is the standard for production SPAs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: gate EE billing/subscription behind is_ee(), rewrite docs, add Bedrock support

- Move billing, subscription, checkin, gift, payment routers behind is_ee() guard
- Gate EE-only startup tasks (subscription roles, FGA tuples, pricing validation)
- Skip consumption tracking (LLM usage, tool calls, settlement) in CE mode
- Return NoopChatLifecycle in CE mode instead of DefaultChatLifecycle
- Fix Bedrock factory to remove consumed credential keys from runtime_kwargs
- Add Bedrock to ProviderType enum migration
- Rewrite docs with product-focused structure (agents, tools, skills, knowledge, etc.)
- Add i18n support with en/zh MDX pages
- Gate gift campaign check behind hasFeature("billing") in frontend
- Exclude /docs from PWA service worker navigateFallbackDenylist

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Meng Junxing <junxingmeng@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(phonehouse): enlace cross-state por rstrip(',') + logging diagnóstico

El enlace anterior por producto_id fallaba si el shop_sku también lleva
comas (cada estado sería un Producto distinto). Ahora se usa

lo que funciona independientemente de si el shop_sku tiene comas o no.

Añadido logging detallado por oferta: base, related_skus, has_buybox,
precios y nuevo_precio para facilitar diagnóstico en Railway.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add v2 coupling API (set_uplift_rate, run_and_get_erosion, apply_drift_correction)

New methods on EnhancedModel:
- _get_mesh_tree(): cached cKDTree (built once, reused every call)
- set_uplift_rate(src_pts, vz_yr): IDW-interpolates DES vertical velocities
  onto GoSPL mesh nodes; stored as _upsub_override for the next run
- run_and_get_erosion(dt, query_pts): snapshots hGlobal, runs GoSPL (with
  skip_tectonics=True if upsub_override is set), computes delta_h on the
  native mesh (no IDW error), then IDW delta_h to query_pts — one pass total
- apply_drift_correction(src_pts, des_elevation, alpha): gently blends
  hGlobal toward DES elevation without a full reset, preserving GoSPL
  drainage network state

Python bridge (gospl_python_interface.py) and C interface (gospl_extensions.h,
gospl_extensions.cpp) updated with corresponding functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update resume with detailed experience bullets and new PDF

Rewrote experience sections with specific metrics and scope (15 teams,

fix: reload ECS agent Docker image after Docker restart in UserData

After nvidia-ctk runtime configure + Docker restart, the overlay2 storage
loses all loaded images. The ECS agent needs its cached image explicitly
reloaded from /var/cache/ecs/ecs-agent.tar before systemctl start ecs.

commit.message:
v3.0: Full rewrite — Teams/TaskList orchestration, 7 parallel points, shared protocols, skill splitting

- Replace hand-rolled pipeline-state.json with native Claude Code Teams/TaskList
- Expand parallelism from 2 to 7 execution points across all phases
- Extract duplicated UX Protocol (13 copies, ~7,800 tokens) into shared protocols (1 copy, ~600 tokens)
- Add conflict resolution protocol with authority hierarchy (security-engineer sole OWASP, sre sole SLO)
- Add input validation protocol with Critical/Degraded/Optional graceful degradation
- Add tool efficiency protocol for parallel reads and smart tools
- Add .production-grade.yaml config layer for existing projects
- Split 6 large skills into router + on-demand phase files (50-80% token savings per invocation)
- Fix code-reviewer: remove security review phase (sole authority is security-engineer)
- Fix devops: remove SLO/runbook generation (sole authority is sre)
- Rewrite orchestrator as thin dispatcher with 13-task dependency graph
- Rewrite all 5 phase dispatchers as minimal coordinators
- Update all 13 SKILL.md files with protocol references and config path support
- Total: 56 files, ~65% reduction in full pipeline token consumption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: dynamic DMR talkgroup fetching from BrandMeister, TGIF, and RadioID

Adds a talkgroup fetch pipeline mirroring the RepeaterBook repeater pipeline.
Fills the radio's 10,000-slot DMR contact list (vs ~30 previously) and uses
actual per-repeater TS1/TS2 TG assignments from RadioID for home-tier channels
instead of hardcoded BrandMeister defaults.

New module: builder/talkgroups.py
- TalkgroupClient: fetches BrandMeister + TGIF registries, cached 7 days
- RadioIDClient: fetches per-repeater static TG lists by state, cached 7 days
- TalkgroupRegistry: merged lookup (name, call_type) with legacy tg_name() fallback

Updated files:
- builder/api.py: RepeaterBook cache TTL 12h → 30 days
- builder/models.py: add Talkgroup dataclass
- builder/zones.py: home_state_channels() uses RadioID TG data when available
- builder/codeplug.py: contact fill in priority order up to radio_max_tgs
- builder/build_config.py: add talkgroups config section (all on by default)
- builder/README.md: pipeline and API reference docs
- screens/build_screen.py: TG fetch steps in build pipeline; clear cache updated
- tool_discovery.py: add max_talkgroups field to RadioProfile (default 10,000)

Tests: 47 new tests in tests/test_talkgroups.py; 408 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feat: add GroqParser with configurable model and fix several issues

Parser:

commit.message:
Epic 271: JIT Tenant & Member Provisioning (#527)

* feat(epic-271): JIT tenant and member provisioning

Add just-in-time provisioning to TenantFilter and MemberFilter for the
Keycloak integration profile. When an unprovisioned org makes its first
request, TenantFilter synchronously provisions the tenant schema using
JWT org claims. MemberFilter enhanced to extract email/name from JWT
standard claims when JIT is enabled, instead of using placeholder values.

- TenantFilter: ObjectProvider<TenantProvisioningService> (lazy, breaks
  circular dep), attemptJitProvisioning with retry-on-race-condition
- MemberFilter: @Value jitProvisioningEnabled, JWT email/name extraction
- ClerkJwtUtils: added extractOrgSlug() for o.slg claim
- application-keycloak.yml: app.jit-provisioning.enabled=true
- 12 new integration tests across 3 test classes

commit.message:
Fix email sending: dynamic jsPDF import to prevent module crash

The top-level jsPDF import caused the entire Edge Function to fail at
startup in Deno's runtime before any try/catch could run, returning a
non-JSON 500 that couldn't be parsed by the client. Moving jsPDF to a
dynamic import inside generatePDF means: if jsPDF loads → PDF attached;
if it fails → caught by caller's try/catch and email sends without PDF.

fix: skip GGUF bounds check for unrecognized quantization types

Models using newer quantization types (e.g. type 39/MXFP4) caused
validation to fail with "unknown quantization type", triggering an
infinite re-download loop. Unknown types are now skipped during bounds
checking since we can't compute their size, but the header parsing
still validates the overall structure.

Add critic calibration: approve-with-notes, resolution staging, convergence memory

Introduces three-way critic decision (approve / approve-with-notes / request-changes)
with resolution_stage on findings (plan vs implementation). Plan-stage blocking findings
gate retries; implementation-stage findings forwarded as guidance to IMPLEMENTER.

Key changes:
- New critic_semantics.py module for normalization/filtering helpers
- Normalization choke point in _run_critic() — invalid stages default to plan,
  implementation+blocking downgraded to warning
- Convergence memory: previous critic report passed on retry with anti-anchoring rules
- Decision enforcement: approve rejected if plan-stage blockers remain
- Implementer context includes grouped critic notes (capped at 20)
- 23 new tests in test_critic_calibration.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(i18n): prevent hero typewriter race condition on index.html

Previous tryUpdate() fired at 4s and 7s — too early for professorName
(~9s) and description (~17s). The typewriter's setInterval kept
appending characters after textContent was overwritten, causing
duplicated/garbled text like "Prof. Yonatan Guerrero Sorianouerrero Soriano".

New approach: poll every 600ms and only replace text once each element
has the 'finished' class (set by TypewriterEffect when animation ends).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden Composio bridge: persistent PATH, Python composio-exec, boot symlinks

Three critical fixes for Composio toolkit reliability:

1. PATH env var: Add /data/bin and /data/node_modules/.bin to machine-level
   PATH so composio-exec and composio CLI survive container restarts without
   needing ephemeral symlinks in /usr/local/bin.

2. Python composio-exec: Replace shell-based script with Python implementation
   that avoids heredoc/quoting issues and adds proper User-Agent header to
   bypass Cloudflare blocks on the Composio API.

3. Boot symlinks: Add skill directory symlink restoration to init cmd so the
   OpenClaw agent discovers the Composio skill after container restarts.

Verified: 11/11 E2E tests pass across Gmail, Google Calendar, GitHub, Linear,
and Notion after gateway restart. 10/10 parallel stress test passes in 1.8s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add multi-window support for opening folders

Extract window management into dedicated service with per-window
workspace path tracking and per-window file system watchers.
Opening a folder when a workspace is already loaded spawns a new window.
